title: Ctenophyllum pectinatum
taxonomy: Ctenophyllum pectinatum is a type of extinct plant that belongs to the family of extinct plants known as Ginkgoales. This family is closely related to the living Ginkgo biloba, and shares some similarities with the conifers. Ctenophyllum pectinatum is classified as a member of the genus Ctenophyllum, which is part of the division of plants called Ginkgoopsida. Further classification places it within the class of seed plants known as Ginkgoopsida and the phylum of plants called Tracheophyta. It is considered to be an ancient plant that dates back to the Mesozoic era.
